Explain the CAP theorem 🔺

The CAP theorem (Brewer's theorem) states that a distributed system can only guarantee two of these three properties simultaneously:

  • Consistency — every read receives the most recent write
  • Availability — every request receives a response (may not be latest)
  • Partition tolerance — the system continues despite network partitions

In practice, network partitions are unavoidable, so real systems must choose between CP (e.g., HBase, Zookeeper) and AP (e.g., DynamoDB, Cassandra).

Show me a simple example of an eventually consistent store

Here's a minimal eventually-consistent key-value store in Python:

class EventualStore:
    def __init__(self):
        self._data = {}
        self._version = {}

    def put(self, key, value):
        self._version[key] = self._version.get(key, 0) + 1
        self._data[key] = (value, self._version[key])

    def get(self, key):
        entry = self._data.get(key)
        return entry[0] if entry else None

    def merge(self, other):
        """Last-write-wins based on version number."""
        for k, (val, ver) in other._data.items():
            own_ver = self._version.get(k, 0)
            if ver > own_ver:
                self._data[k] = (val, ver)
                self._version[k] = ver
